---
title: "Bayesian coverage modeling"
format:
  html:
    link-external-icon: true
    link-external-newwindow: true
---

Welcome to the website of the Countdown Bayesian coverage modeling project!

The Countdown Bayesian coverage modeling project began in 2024 as a statistical modeling capacity strengthening and analysis project. The goal of the project was to develop statistical models for estimating and forecasting Countdown coverage indicators. We focused on a general class of Bayesian hierarchical temporal models that includes FPET, a model that is used for estimating and forecasting family planning indicators such as modern contraceptive use, based on survey and routine data. More information about FPET is available on the next page.

The project included a training component designed to build participants' understanding of Bayesian models and their application to coverage indicator estimation and forecasting at national and subnational levels. The training covered conceptual foundations of Bayesian modeling, data exploration, and model fitting using the open-source software R and Stan. Training materials are available on this website.

We developed a model to estimate and forecast coverage indicators using survey data and routine data. Our modeling approach is available in R packages and included in the Countdown data suite. More information is provided on this website.

Contact: Leontine Alkema (lalkema\@umass.edu).
